message:
refactor(cx): migrate NameRegistry from BTreeMap to DetHashMap with explicit sort guarantees

NameRegistry previously used BTreeMap for all six internal maps (leases,
pending, waiters, granted, watchers_by_ref/name/region) to get
deterministic iteration order "for free". However, most operations
(lookup, insert, remove) only need O(1) access, and the sorted iteration
was only actually required by two public methods: `registered_names()`
and `cleanup_region()`/`cleanup_task()`.

This commit replaces all six BTreeMaps with DetHashMap (deterministic
hash map using a fixed-seed hasher from crate::util) for O(1) average
lookup, and adds explicit `sort_unstable()` in the two places where
deterministic output ordering is part of the API contract:

- `registered_names()` now collects keys and sorts before returning
- `cleanup_region()` and `cleanup_task()` already sorted their output

All maps are pre-allocated with capacity hints matching expected working
set sizes (32 for leases, 16 for pending/waiters/watchers, 8 for
watchers_by_region).

Test comments updated throughout to reference "API contract" and
"deterministic lexical order" instead of "BTreeMap guarantee", making
the ordering invariant explicit rather than implementation-coupled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
